text: Stepping into this space, the mix of bold color and abstract geometry draws my attention outward, yet the hum of technology and the language of 'R+D' urges reflection inward. I find myself aware not just of what is exhibited, but of my own presence as a subject of observation. There is a strange comfort in the silence, but also a quiet tension, as though the room itself asks me to consider what it means to watch and be watched, to hide and reveal. I feel curious and slightly unsettled, as if I'm both a participant and the object of someone else's experiment.
